subject: Human Resources
title: Guilds and Early Worker Organizations
description: Learn Guilds and Early Worker Organizations in one minute.

hook.text: In medieval Europe, if you wanted to be a blacksmith, you didn't apply for a job—you joined a guild.

buildup.text: Guilds controlled entry into trades, set quality standards, and provided training through apprenticeships.

discovery.text: They were the earliest form of worker organization—protecting members' interests and regulating the market.

twist.text: Guilds also restricted competition. They limited who could work, keeping wages high for members at others' expense.

climax.text: Guilds faded with industrialization, but their DNA lives on in trade unions and professional associations.

punchline.text: Guilds were the first HR departments—run by workers.

quiz.question: What role did medieval guilds play?
options: Controlled trade entry, standards, and training
Only collected taxes for the king
